draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to friendly, encouraging tone than technical/formal instruction
facade.label: Casual coach voice
facade.acceptImplies: Warm, conversational language; celebratory alerts; personal encouragement in UI copy
facade.rejectImplies: Objective, data-first tone; minimal copy; clinical readouts of metrics

evidence.hypothesis: Runners respond better to friendly, encouraging tone than technical/formal instruction
evidence.implication: Warm, conversational language; celebratory alerts; personal encouragement in UI copy

facade.hypothesis: A step counter that animates with each footfall—bouncing up and down—rather than static number updates
facade.label: Bouncing pace indicator
facade.acceptImplies: Runner wants tactile, rhythmic visual reinforcement of their movement; fluid micro-animations build momentum and engagement
facade.rejectImplies: Runner prioritizes glanceability and information density; animation feels distracting or adds cognitive load during physical exertion

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from organic, energizing warmth—golden/orange tones that feel motivating rather than clinical. This tests whether the mood skews toward natural, biological comfort or sleek digital efficiency.
facade.axisTargeted: Warm vs. cool; organic vs. digital
facade.label: Warm sunrise palette
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward inviting, human-centered interfaces that feel alive and inspiring—not sterile. Likely favors imagery, gradients, approachability.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer stark, minimal, data-forward design—cool grays, high contrast, functional coolness. Or believes a weather app should feel authoritative, not emotional.

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar showing current temp, wind, pace zones rather than hiding metrics in tabs or headers
facade.label: Sidebar with live stats
facade.acceptImplies: Runner wants glanceable, always-present metrics during activity; favors compact side real estate over modal or tab switching
facade.rejectImplies: Runner prefers full-screen map/route focus; metrics should hide or appear only on swipe; favors immersion over dashboard density

facade.hypothesis: Runners want real-time data (pace, HR, distance) immediately visible alongside their route—not hidden behind taps
facade.label: Split screen: metrics left, map right
facade.content: Split screen: metrics left, map right
facade.acceptImplies: User values simultaneous access to numbers and spatial context; build dual-pane layouts for information density
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers single-focus view (either data dashboard OR map dominance); consider sequential or modal presentation

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners may benefit from high-contrast, vibrant UI elements (neon colors against darker backgrounds) to feel energetic and motivating, matching runner psychology.
facade.axisTargeted: Color intensity & saturation — are we going muted/natural or bold/electric?
facade.label: Bright neon accents
facade.acceptImplies: Users want visual energy. Explore neon greens, electric blues, hot pinks on dark or light modes. Consider high-contrast CTAs for pace/distance triggers.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer calm, restful visual language. Explore pastels, desaturated tones, or nature-inspired color palettes (sky blues, earth grays). Avoid overstimulation.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, zen atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented mood for pre-run prep
Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, UV, wind gusts)
Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback when checking conditions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets vs. deep-dive menu navigation for weather info during runs
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, zen atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented mood for pre-run prep
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes simple essentials (temp, rain) vs. detailed metrics (humidity, UV, wind gusts)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback when checking conditions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners seeking performance gains prefer direct, commanding tone over gentle encouragement
facade.label: Drill sergeant mode
facade.acceptImplies: Build assertive, goal-focused copy; use imperatives; emphasize metrics and intensity
facade.rejectImplies: Stick with warm, celebratory voice; focus on positive reinforcement and companionship

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:

Good for Running

Mild temps, light wind. Best window: next 2 hours.
